sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 38, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'RIGHT': 'Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED step 8', 'LEFT': 'Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED step 2', 'UP': 'Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED step 4', 'DOWN': 'Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED step 6', 'MOUSE': 'Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center) VERIFIED step 7'}
- Object taxonomy: {'g': 'gray background', 'w': 'white panels/squares', 'p': 'purple vertical bar (barrier between panels)', 'N': 'light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)', 'c': 'charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)', 'W': 'white single pixel (selected token center)', 'B': 'black single pixel (unselected token center)'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'row 63, charcoal strip'}
- Win pattern: {'rule': 'Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame. Select frame with MOUSE, then use arrow keys to move it.'}
Current level:
- World model: Left frame in left target (rows 33-35, cols 12-14). Right frame selected (white center) at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. Purple barrier at cols 33-38, rows 21-41 (continuous, no gaps). Right target at rows 26-30, cols 44-48 (behind barrier). Frame moves 3 cells per arrow key press. Frame can only move on white panel areas.
- Goal model: Move right frame to right target (rows 26-30, cols 44-48). Barrier blocks direct RIGHT movement. Need to find alternate path or mechanic.
- Recent findings: ['Left frame successfully placed in left target', 'Right frame can move LEFT/RIGHT within left panel', 'Barrier at cols 33-38 is continuous from rows 21-41', 'Frame cannot move on gray background areas']
- Open questions: ['How to get right frame past purple barrier to right target?', 'Is there a way to move the barrier or create a gap?', 'Can the left frame be moved out of its target to let right frame use it?']
- Plan: Try moving right frame RIGHT again from cols 27-29 to see if it can reach cols 30-32 and then through barrier. If blocked, consider moving left frame out of target and swapping positions.
- Strategy log: ['Attempted to move right frame directly RIGHT through barrier: blocked at cols 30-32', 'Attempted UP/DOWN on right frame: blocked by gray areas above and below corridor']
- Failed probes: ['UP on right frame at rows 30-32: no movement (step 24)', 'RIGHT on right frame at cols 30-32: no movement, barrier blocked (step 25)', 'DOWN on right frame at rows 30-32: no movement (step 26)', 'LEFT on right frame: moved back to cols 27-29 (step 27, not failed but retreated)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 37 actions; a strong score needs about 28 or fewer. You are 1.3x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! The board changed (`board_changed: true`). The left frame moved RIGHT from cols 30-32 to cols 33-35. But wait, the barrier is at cols 33-38! So the frame moved INTO the barrier area. Looking at the image, I can see the left frame (white center) is now overlapping with the purple barrier. It seems like the frame CAN pass through the barrier at rows 30-32!
This is a key finding. The barrier has a gap at rows 30-32, which is exactly where the corridor is. The left frame is now at cols 33-35, which is inside the barrier area.
Let me continue moving RIGHT to get the left frame to the right panel, and then to the right target.
Actually, wait. Let me look at the image more carefully. The left frame (white center) is now at the edge of the purple barrier, partially overlapping it. The right frame (black center) is still at cols 27-29.
So the left frame can pass through the barrier at rows 30-32! This is the corridor height. Let me continue moving RIGHT to get the left frame to the right panel.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Left frame moved RIGHT into barrier at rows 30-32! Barrier has a gap at corridor height!
# Continue moving RIGHT to get to right panel
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
n_frames = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
for n in n_frames:
    b = n['boundary']
    print(f"N frame: boundary={b}, children={n['children']}")

# Left frame is now at cols 33-35 (inside barrier). Continue RIGHT to reach right panel
# Right target is at rows 26-30, cols 44-48
# Need to move RIGHT several more times, then UP to align with target
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active green frame right by 3 cols VERIFIED step 8", "LEFT": "Moves active green frame left by 3 cols VERIFIED step 2", "UP": "Moves active green frame up by 3 rows VERIFIED step 4", "DOWN": "Moves active green frame down by 3 rows VERIFIED step 6", "MOUSE": "Clicking on green frame selects it (gives white center) VERIFIED step 7"}, "object_taxonomy": {"g": "gray background", "w": "white panels/squares", "p": "purple vertical bar", "N": "light green 3x3 frames (movable tokens)", "c": "charcoal 5x5 frames (targets)", "W": "white single pixel (selected token center)", "B": "black single pixel (unselected token center)"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "row 63, charcoal strip"}, "win_pattern": {"rule": "Move each green frame into a charcoal target frame"}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Left frame selected at rows 30-32, cols 33-35 (inside barrier). Right frame at rows 30-32, cols 27-29. Barrier has gap at corridor height (rows 30-32)!", "goal_model": "Move left frame through barrier to right target, then move right frame to left target", "recent_findings": ["Barrier has gap at rows 30-32 (corridor height)", "Frames can pass through barrier at corridor height"], "open_questions": ["Can left frame reach right target from here?"], "current_plan": "Move left frame RIGHT through barrier to right panel, then position in right target", "strategy_log": ["Barrier blocks at rows 33-35 but not at rows 30-32"], "failed_probes": ["UP on right frame", "DOWN on right frame", "RIGHT on right frame at cols 30-32", "RIGHT on left frame at rows 33-35: blocked by barrier"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
